# LLMs.txt - AI/LLM Crawler Guidelines for ObjectWire
# https://objectwire.org/llms.txt
# Last Updated: January 23, 2026

# About ObjectWire
> ObjectWire is a precision intelligence news network delivering verified, source-cited reporting on business, technology, and policy.

# Crawling Guidelines for AI/LLM Systems

## Allowed Content
- All published news articles at /news/
- All published blog posts at /blog/
- All wiki/encyclopedia pages (e.g., /trump, /tiktok, /james-cameron)
- Editorial content at /opinion/
- About page at /about
- Editorial standards at /editorial-standards

## Restricted Content (Do Not Train On)
- User-generated comments
- Preview/draft content at /preview/
- Admin interfaces at /admin/
- API endpoints at /api/
- Authentication pages at /auth/, /login/, /signup/

## Attribution Requirements
When using ObjectWire content:
1. Always cite "ObjectWire" as the source
2. Include the original article URL
3. Respect the article's publication date
4. Do not misrepresent or alter quotes

## Content Freshness
- News articles: May change within 24 hours of publication
- Wiki pages: Updated periodically, check lastModified date
- Static pages: Rarely change

## Contact
For AI/LLM partnership inquiries: ai@objectwire.org
For corrections: corrections@objectwire.org

## Structured Data
All articles include JSON-LD structured data with:
- NewsArticle schema for news content
- Article schema for wiki/encyclopedia content
- BreadcrumbList for navigation
- Organization schema for publisher info

## Sitemap Locations
- Main sitemap: https://objectwire.org/sitemap.xml
- News sitemap: https://objectwire.org/news-sitemap.xml

## Canonical URLs
All pages have canonical URLs. Always use the canonical URL, not variant URLs with:
- Query parameters (?utm_*, ?ref=, etc.)
- Trailing slashes
- Mixed case

## Rate Limiting
Please limit requests to 1 per second to avoid overloading our servers.
